description: Team.Id has a public setter (`public Guid Id { get; set; }`), making the entity's identity mutable after construction. The TeamRepository keys its internal dictionary on `team.Id` at the time of `Add`; if a caller mutates `team.Id` afterward, the stored key and the object's own Id diverge, making `GetById(team.Id)` return null for the modified team. A repository contract that depends on stable identity requires the Id to be init-only or read-only.
suggestedFix: Change the property to `public Guid Id { get; init; } = Guid.NewGuid();` so the Id can only be set at object-initializer time and is immutable thereafter.

description: `Update` checks `_teams.ContainsKey(team.Id)` and then sets `_teams[team.Id] = team` as two separate dictionary operations within the same lock. This is safe under the lock, but using `_teams.ContainsKey` + assignment instead of a single `TryGetValue` or a pattern that makes the intent explicit is inconsistent with the `Add` method which correctly uses `TryAdd`. Minor consistency/clarity issue.
suggestedFix: Refactor to: `if (!_teams.ContainsKey(team.Id)) throw new KeyNotFoundException(...); _teams[team.Id] = team;` is fine as-is, but optionally use a guard-and-assign pattern matching the style of `Add` for consistency.
